title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Inspector Bartholomew introduces Monsieur Le Mon, a spy from the Paris Security Bureau, to a group including Chief Balthar, Virginia Rue, Mr. Everly, and Mr. Kade. Virginia Rue, whose husband served in the diplomatic service during the assassination of the King and Queen of Hesse, requests full disclosure of the situation. Chief Balthar agrees, noting that the matter involves secrets previously kept by Roamax.

As the group gathers around an oak table, Chief Balthar explains that the current crisis stems from secret operations orchestrated seven years ago by the Earl of Stapleton. Le Mon reveals that a professional thief known as "King Victor" once operated in Paris under the alias Captain O'Neill. Le Mon connects this thief to Yvesse L'Immer, an actress from the White Hot Troupe. The Red Hand Party had originally hired L'Immer to seduce King Nicholas IV of Hesse to facilitate a political plot, but L'Immer instead seduced the King and became Queen Farajka of Hesse by posing as a relative of the Romanov nobility.

Following the assassination of the King and Queen by the Red Hand Party, it was discovered that many royal jewels had been replaced with counterfeits. Le Mon explains that Queen Farajka maintained contact with King Victor using coded English letters signed with the name "Virginia Rue" to avoid suspicion. Chief Balthar states that while the Queen hid a specific jewel within Britain, its location remains unknown, though he suggests it may be in the room. Le Mon adds that during the chaos of the Hersovsky Revolution, Captain O'Neill was arrested in Paris, and the secret letters were stolen by emissaries from Hersovsky.
